events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: Sir Richard Starkey, known professionally as Ringo Starr, was born on July 7, 1940, and rose to international fame as the drummer for The Beatles.

angles.name: Drumming Legend
angles.copy: Eighty-six years young and still a drumming legend! Happy Birthday to the one and only Ringo Starr, whose beat shaped a generation.

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Sir Brian Harold May, born on July 19, 1947, is the legendary lead guitarist of the rock band Queen, renowned for his distinctive sound and songwriting.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Happy 79th Birthday to the incredible Brian May! His iconic guitar work with Queen continues to inspire generations of musicians.

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Sir Michael Philip Jagger, born on July 26, 1943, is the charismatic lead singer and a founding member of The Rolling Stones, known for his energetic performances.

angles.name: Rock Icon
angles.copy: Still rocking stages worldwide at 83! Wishing a very Happy Birthday to the legendary Mick Jagger. What's your favourite Stones track?

events.title: MTV launched (1981)
events.blurb: On August 1, 1981, MTV officially launched, revolutionising the music industry with its 24-hour music video programming, famously kicking off with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star'.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: Forty-five years ago, MTV changed music forever. Remember the first video ever played? It was The Buggles declaring 'Video Killed The Radio Star'. Irony, much?

events.title: Oasis released Definitely Maybe (1994)
events.blurb: Oasis released their iconic debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, which quickly became a landmark of the Britpop era.

angles.name: Britpop Classic
angles.copy: Thirty-two years ago, Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and Britpop was never the same. What's your top track from this era-defining album?

events.title: Woodstock Festival took place (1969)
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal event in music history, took place from August 15-18, 1969, in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Peace & Music
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, half a million people gathered for '3 Days of Peace & Music' at Woodstock. What an incredible moment in cultural history.

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's Death (1977)
events.blurb: August 16, 2026, marks the 49th anniversary of the passing of Elvis Presley, the King of Rock 'n' Roll, who died on August 16, 1977.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: Remembering the King of Rock 'n' Roll, Elvis Presley, 49 years after his passing. His music continues to define generations.

events.title: The Beatles released Abbey Road (1969)
events.blurb: The Beatles' eleventh studio album, 'Abbey Road', was released in the UK on September 26, 1969, becoming one of their most iconic and best-selling records.

angles.name: Iconic Album
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years since The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That album cover is still one of the most famous in music history.

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, born on September 7, 1951, is the formidable lead singer, songwriter, and guitarist of The Pretenders.

angles.name: Pretenders Frontwoman
angles.copy: Happy 75th Birthday to the iconic Chrissie Hynde! Her distinctive voice and rock 'n' roll attitude have kept The Pretenders at the forefront for decades.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: William John Paul Gallagher, born on September 21, 1972, is the distinctive lead singer of Oasis and a successful solo artist.

angles.name: Rock 'n' Roll Star
angles.copy: Happy 54th Birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! Still bringing that unmistakable swagger and voice to the world.

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' released (1971)
events.blurb: John Lennon's timeless anthem 'Imagine' was released as a single in the US on October 11, 1971, with the album following shortly after.

angles.name: Timeless Message
angles.copy: Fifty-five years since John Lennon released 'Imagine'. Its message of peace and unity resonates just as strongly today.

events.title: Apple released the first iPod (2001)
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the first-generation iPod on October 23, 2001, revolutionising portable music with its '1,000 songs in your pocket' promise.

angles.name: Music in your Pocket
angles.copy: Twenty-five years ago, the first iPod dropped and changed how we listen to music forever. Remember your first one?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th is celebrated annually as James Bond Day, marking the anniversary of the world premiere of the first Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: 007 Day
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Celebrating 64 years of 007, martinis (shaken, not stirred), and iconic theme tunes. Who's your favourite Bond?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: John Winston Ono Lennon, born on October 9, 1940, was a co-founder of The Beatles and a legendary songwriter and peace activist.

angles.name: Lennon's Legacy
angles.copy: Remembering John Lennon on what would have been his 86th birthday. His music and message of peace continue to inspire the world.
